title: 5 times the Patriots traded for wide receivers in recent history, and how each acquisition panned out
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Patriots traded for Randy Moss in 2007 from the Raiders for a fourth-round pick, leading to record-setting performances.
New England acquired Wes Welker from the Dolphins in 2007, enhancing their roster despite not earning a Super Bowl win.
Josh Gordon joined the Patriots in 2018, showing potential but was hindered by off-field issues, affecting his stay.

title: Alex Cora not harping on late-game ejection by C.B. Bucknor: ‘We’re all human’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora was ejected for arguing a controversial check swing strike call involving Trevor Story against the Reds.
Cora expressed disappointment Bucknor didn't appeal to the first base umpire and acknowledged human error in officiating.
Boston's Roman Anthony criticized himself for a failed ABS challenge that left the team without the ability to contest further calls.

title: Trevor Story reacts to strike three call that got Alex Cora ejected in Red Sox’ loss
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Trevor Story criticized umpire CB Bucknor's strike three call, which led to Alex Cora's ejection during Red Sox's 6-5 loss.
Story disagreed with Bucknor's decision not to consult the first base umpire on his check swing strikeout.
The Red Sox tied the game but lost in the 11th inning, with the team now holding a 1-1 record.

title: Brimming with motivation after missing Olympics, Pavel Zacha is playing his best hockey for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Pavel Zacha scored 11 goals in 16 games after missing the Olympics due to a concussion.
Zacha reached a career-high 26 goals this season, helping the Bruins as their second-line center.
Coach Marco Sturm moved Zacha to center, boosting the Bruins' offensive and defensive performance.

title: Here’s what Red Sox SP Sonny Gray said about his rough debut
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray's Red Sox debut ended with a no-decision in a 6-5 loss against the Cincinnati Reds.
Gray allowed four runs in four innings, including a solo home run by Reds first baseman Sal Stewart.
Red Sox manager Alex Cora said Gray finished strong and praised the bullpen's performance after Gray's exit.

title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Tens of thousands gathered at Boston Common for the third 'No Kings' protest organized by local advocacy groups.
Musical performances and speeches from leaders like Gov. Maura Healey and Sen. Elizabeth Warren highlighted the event.
Over 3,300 protests were held nationwide, with more than 160 in Massachusetts, emphasizing issues like immigration and anti-war activism.

title: Review & setlist: GWAR gives fanbase a bloody good time at House of Blues
author: Christopher Hurley
bullets: GWAR performed a 90-minute set at the House of Blues with heavy metal theatrics and fake blood.
The concert featured 17 songs, including a cover of Chappell Roan's 'Pink Pony Club' as a tribute to a late member.
The band's performance included social and political satire, targeting figures like Donald Trump and ICE agents.

title: Celtics post lengthy injury report ahead of Sunday’s Hornets matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum are questionable for the Celtics' game against the Hornets on Sunday.
Jayson Tatum has struggled with shooting but has maintained significant playtime since returning earlier this month.
Nikola Vucevic is ruled out due to a right ring finger fracture for the game against Charlotte.

title: ‘No Kings’ protest: What protesters said and why they showed up
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Thousands gathered on Boston Common for the 'No Kings' protest, part of over 160 rallies in Massachusetts.
Key speakers included Gov. Maura Healey, Sen. Elizabeth Warren, and Sen. Ed Markey, with performances by Dropkick Murphys.
Protesters voiced concerns about democracy, nationalism, and war, using costumes and signs to express their messages.

title: Photo scroll: Scenes from Boston’s ‘No Kings’ protest on March 28
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Thousands gathered for 'No Kings' protest on Boston Common on March 28, part of over 160 events in Massachusetts.
Governor Maura Healey and Senators Edward Markey and Elizabeth Warren addressed the crowd during the Boston rally.
Protesters walked through the Public Garden and Boston Common, highlighting concerns over political leadership and foreign policy.
